papers.title: SLAI T-Rex: Full-Parameter Post-training of the DeepSeek-V4 Family on Ascend SuperPOD
papers.abstract: Abstract  Full-parameter post-training of trillion-parameter-scale MoE models introduces substantial system-level challenges for large-scale distributed training, including severe memory pressure, non-overlapped communication overhead, and inefficient kernel execution. While most large-scale LLM training systems are built around GPU-based clusters, this report presents an end-to-end optimization practice on the Ascend NPU SuperPOD. Using the DeepSeek-V4 model family as the target workload, we develop a hierarchical optimization framework spanning model-level parallelism, computation-communication orchestration, and low-level kernel execution. The resulting system achieves 34.22% Model FLOPs Utilization (MFU) with a 2.93x improvement over the open-source baseline recipe while maintaining training stability. Building on this optimized infrastructure, we further establish a CPT and SFT workflow for complex Operations Research (OR) tasks. We refer to the integrated framework as SLAI T-Rex. Using DeepSeek-V4-Flash, we develop OR-oriented CPT and SFT data pipelines that combine collected domain resources with solver-verified synthetic optimization documents. The resulting dataset contains 10K high-quality SFT samples spanning four task categories and three problem representations. The specialized model achieves the highest average zero-shot Pass@1 score among the evaluated models, reaching 71.81% and outperforming GPT-5.4-Mini and the base DeepSeek-V4-Flash model by 3.98 and 11.27 percentage points, respectively. Overall, this work demonstrates a full-stack pathway from efficient trillion-parameter model post-training on Ascend infra to domain-specialized Flash models for solver-grounded mathematical modeling, advancing frontier-model systems for complex reasoning.

ru.title: От триллионов параметров к точным решениям: полный стек оптимизации для специализированных LLM
ru.desc: В работе представлена комплексная система оптимизации для дообучения триллионопараметрических моделей смеси экспертов (MoE) на ускорителях Ascend NPU, включающая иерархическую оптимизацию параллелизма моделей и оркестрации вычислений. Авторы разработали фреймворк SLAI T-Rex, который достигает 34.22% Model FLOPs Utilization и 2.93x улучшения по сравнению с базовыми решениями, при этом сохраняя стабильность обучения. На основе оптимизированной инфраструктуры были созданы конвейеры предварительного обучения (CPT) и настройки (SFT) для задач исследования операций, объединяющие собранные доменные ресурсы с синтетическими документами, верифицированными решателями. Специализированная модель DeepSeek-V4-Flash достигла лучшего результата 71.81% в задачах zero-shot, превзойдя базовую модель на 11.27 процентных пункта и демонстрируя эффективность полного стека обучения от оптимизации больших моделей до решения сложных задач математического моделирования.

en.title: Optimizing Trillion-Parameter Models for Complex Reasoning
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges of training large-scale Mixture of Experts (MoE) models, particularly those with trillions of parameters, by optimizing the training process on Ascend NPU SuperPOD. It introduces a hierarchical optimization framework that enhances model-level parallelism and improves computation-communication efficiency, achieving significant gains in Model FLOPs Utilization. The authors also develop a specialized workflow for Operations Research tasks, resulting in a high-quality dataset and a model that outperforms existing benchmarks. Overall, the work showcases advancements in training efficiency and model performance for complex reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 高效万亿参数模型的后训练与优化
zh.desc: 本文探讨了在Ascend NPU SuperPOD上对万亿参数规模的MoE模型进行全参数后训练所面临的系统级挑战，包括内存压力、通信开销和内核执行效率低下。我们提出了一种分层优化框架，涵盖模型级并行性、计算与通信的协调以及低级内核执行，从而实现了34.22%的模型FLOPs利用率，并在保持训练稳定性的同时，较开源基线提高了2.93倍。基于这一优化基础，我们建立了针对复杂运筹学任务的CPT和SFT工作流，并开发了结合领域资源和求解器验证的合成优化文档的数据管道。最终，专门化模型在评估模型中实现了71.81%的最高平均零-shot Pass@1分数，超越了GPT-5.4-Mini和基础DeepSeek-V4-Flash模型。

papers.title: Self Gradient Forcing: Native Long Video Extrapolation
papers.abstract: Abstract  Recent autoregressive video diffusion methods are increasingly built upon Self Forcing, where the student is trained on histories produced by its own rollout rather than ground-truth video contexts. This reduces exposure bias, but the historical key-value cache is still used by future frames only as frozen rollout state. As a result, future losses cannot supervise how earlier generated latents should be written into more useful keys and values for later video-latent generation. We call this the historical context-gradient gap. We propose Self Gradient Forcing (SGF), a two-pass training strategy that restores this missing supervision signal without backpropagating through the full serial rollout. Pass 1 performs a no-gradient autoregressive rollout matching inference and, at a sampled denoising exit step, records both the self-generated context and the noisy latents fed to the model. Pass 2 performs parallel context-gradient reconstruction for the recorded exit step. The generated context is used as stop-gradient clean-latent input, while the model recomputes the context KV representations and future-to-context causal attention. Thus, SGF provides the missing memory-writing supervision within the native autoregressive training objective, using losses on future video latents to train the model to encode context into more effective causal memory. Across extensive long-horizon frame-wise and chunk-wise experiments under different initializations, SGF achieves stronger native long-video extrapolation than Self Forcing, especially in subject identity, background/layout consistency, and temporal stability. Remarkably, using only a 5-second training window, SGF can extrapolate to videos lasting several minutes. Code and models will be released to advance research on autoregressive video generation.

ru.title: Восстановление градиентов памяти в авторегрессивной генерации видео
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод Self Gradient Forcing (SGF) для обучения авторегрессивных диффузионных моделей видео, который решает проблему отсутствия градиентов для оптимизации памяти контекста. Авторы используют двухпроходную стратегию: первый проход выполняет авторегрессивный откат без градиентов, второй проход восстанавливает градиенты для переобучения представлений ключей и значений контекста. Метод позволяет будущим потерям контролировать, как ранее сгенерированные скрытые состояния должны кодироваться в полезную память для последующей генерации видеокадров. Экспериментально показано, что SGF значительно превосходит базовый Self Forcing в экстраполяции длинных видео, обеспечивая стабильность объектов, фона и временной согласованности при обучении на 5-секундных фрагментах.

en.title: Enhancing Video Generation with Self Gradient Forcing
en.desc: This paper introduces Self Gradient Forcing (SGF), a new training method for autoregressive video generation that addresses the historical context-gradient gap. Traditional methods use past generated frames as fixed context, which limits their ability to improve future frame generation. SGF employs a two-pass strategy: the first pass generates video frames without gradients, while the second pass reconstructs context gradients to enhance memory writing. This approach allows the model to better encode useful information for future frames, resulting in improved video quality and consistency over longer durations.

zh.title: 自梯度强制：提升视频生成的有效性
zh.desc: 最近的自回归视频扩散方法越来越多地基于自我强制（Self Forcing），即学生模型在自己生成的历史数据上进行训练，而不是在真实视频上下文上。这种方法减少了暴露偏差，但历史的关键值缓存仍然只作为冻结的生成状态供未来帧使用。因此，未来的损失无法监督早期生成的潜在变量如何写入更有用的键和值。我们提出了自梯度强制（Self Gradient Forcing，SGF），这是一种两次训练策略，能够在不通过完整序列回传的情况下恢复缺失的监督信号。

papers.title: Beyond Relevance-Centric Retrieval: Rubric-Oriented Document Set Selection and Ranking
papers.abstract: Abstract  As large language models and AI agents become the primary consumers of search results, document set quality determines the upper bound of downstream generation. Yet existing evaluation systems remain confined to scoring documents independently and aggregating via nDCG, ignoring inter-document interactions (redundancy, conflict, complementarity) and unable to answer what makes one document set better than another. To address these issues, we propose a complete evaluate-diagnose-optimize framework. We design SetwiseEvalKit, a three-level, nine-dimension document set evaluation benchmark covering both short-form and long-form scenarios, comprising approximately 28K high-quality evaluation rubrics. We systematically evaluate 12 rerankers: even the best method achieves no more than 45% coverage, cross-document coordination dimensions are universally weak, and no single method maintains top performance across both settings. Building on this, we propose Rubric4Setwise, a training-free method that converts rubric-based evaluation criteria into document set selection signals, achieving the best downstream generation performance with fewer documents and search rounds. It is the only method that maintains state-of-the-art results across both scenarios, validating the effectiveness of closing the loop from evaluation to optimization.
affiliations: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: От оценки наборов документов к оптимизации поиска для LLM
ru.desc: В работе предложена комплексная система для оценки качества наборов документов, которые используются AI-агентами и большими языковыми моделями. Авторы разработали SetwiseEvalKit — многоуровневый бенчмарк с девятью измерениями для оценки наборов документов, включающий около 28 тысяч высококачественных критериев оценки. Выполнена систематическая оценка 12 методов переранжирования, показавшая универсальные слабости в координации между документами. Предложен метод Rubric4Setwise без обучения, который преобразует критерии оценки в сигналы для отбора документов, достигая лучшей производительности при уменьшенном количестве документов.

en.title: Optimizing Document Sets for Better AI Performance
en.desc: This paper addresses the limitations of current document evaluation systems used in machine learning, particularly for large language models and AI agents. It highlights that existing methods score documents independently, failing to consider how documents interact with each other, which affects the quality of search results. The authors introduce a new framework called SetwiseEvalKit, which evaluates document sets based on multiple dimensions and scenarios, providing a more comprehensive assessment. They also present Rubric4Setwise, a novel method that optimizes document selection based on evaluation criteria, achieving superior performance in generating results with fewer documents.

zh.title: 优化文档集评估，提升生成质量
zh.desc: 随着大型语言模型和人工智能代理成为搜索结果的主要消费者，文档集的质量决定了下游生成的上限。现有的评估系统仅独立评分文档，并通过nDCG聚合，忽视了文档之间的相互作用（冗余、冲突、互补性），无法回答为什么一个文档集比另一个更好。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一个完整的评估-诊断-优化框架，并设计了SetwiseEvalKit，这是一个涵盖短文和长文场景的三层九维文档集评估基准。我们还提出了Rubric4Setwise，这是一种无训练的方法，将基于评估标准的评估标准转化为文档集选择信号，能够在更少的文档和搜索轮次中实现最佳的下游生成性能。

papers.title: An Exam for Active Observers
papers.abstract: Abstract  Human vision is a closed loop: gaze is continuously redirected by intermediate hypotheses rather than a single snapshot. Decades of psychophysics and cognitive science have argued that this active observation is essential for a wide range of tasks. Whether today's multimodal large language models (MLLMs) exercise active observation is an empirical question that current vision-language benchmarks do not answer. We introduce ActiveVision, a benchmark that makes active observation measurable for MLLMs, comprising 17 tasks across 3 categories. Tasks are designed to force repeated visual perception rather than a single static description. Frontier MLLMs collapse on ActiveVision: the highest-scoring model we evaluate, GPT-5.5 at the highest exposed reasoning-effort tier, solves only 10.6% of items and scores zero on 11 of the 17 tasks, and even Claude Fable 5, despite topping most reasoning and coding leaderboards, solves just 3.5%, far behind three human participants who average 96.1%. Furthermore, much of the gap persists even when models write and run their own vision code: such code is unreliable on realistic imagery, and catching its failures itself requires the active perception the models lack. Together, these results indicate that current MLLMs lack robust active visual observation, motivating architectures and training objectives that close the perception-reasoning loop.

ru.title: Активное зрение: замыкание контура между восприятием и рассуждением в мультимодальных моделях
ru.desc: В статье предложен бенчмарк ActiveVision для оценки способности мультимодальных больших языковых моделей (MLLM) к активному наблюдению — многократному переправлению внимания на визуальную сцену, как это делает человеческое зрение. Исследование показало, что современные MLLM критически неэффективны на этих задачах: GPT-4.5 решает только 10.6% заданий, а Claude Fable 5 — всего 3.5%, тогда как люди достигают 96.1% точности. Даже когда модели генерируют и выполняют собственный компьютерный код для анализа изображений, их решения часто ненадежны на реальных данных. Авторы заключают, что текущим MLLM не хватает робастного механизма активного визуального восприятия, что требует разработки новых архитектур и методов обучения.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Enhancing Active Observation in MLLMs
en.desc: This paper investigates whether modern multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can perform active observation, a process crucial for human vision. The authors introduce a new benchmark called ActiveVision, which includes 17 tasks designed to require repeated visual perception rather than just a single observation. The results show that leading MLLMs, like GPT-5.5 and Claude Fable 5, struggle significantly on these tasks, achieving low success rates compared to human participants. The findings suggest that current MLLMs do not effectively engage in active visual observation, highlighting the need for improved model architectures and training methods to enhance their perception-reasoning capabilities.

zh.title: 提升机器视觉：主动观察的必要性
zh.desc: 本文探讨了人类视觉的闭环特性，强调主动观察在多种任务中的重要性。我们提出了ActiveVision基准，旨在衡量多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）的主动观察能力，包含17个任务，分为3个类别。实验结果显示，当前的前沿MLLMs在ActiveVision基准上表现不佳，最高得分的模型仅解决了10.6%的任务，远低于人类参与者的平均96.1%。这些结果表明，现有的MLLMs缺乏强大的主动视觉观察能力，提示需要改进模型架构和训练目标，以缩小感知与推理之间的差距。

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Hypernetwork-Based Knowledge Injection in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract  Injecting factual knowledge into large language models (LLMs) reliably and at scale remains an open challenge. Hypernetworks provide a promising solution to large-scale knowledge injection. Although hypernetworks are typically applied for test-time adaptation, we explore their use in train-time knowledge injection, where, given a large corpus of facts, we train a hypernetwork to generate a fixed LoRA adapter that, when inserted into the target model, enable the model to answer questions about those facts. In this work, we investigate whether hypernetworks can be used to perform train-time knowledge injection and how this ability varies with scale. The scaling behavior of hypernetworks remains largely unstudied. Our design decouples the hypernetwork's injection capacity from the target model's general capability, enabling, for the first time, a rigorous study of scaling laws for hypernetwork architectures. We characterize how loss, reasoning accuracy, and out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization vary with hypernetwork depth, width, and target network size. We construct a large-scale dataset, called MegaWikiQA, containing tens of millions of multi-hop question-answer examples across 39 domains constructed from examples in Wikidata5M. Our results reveal: (i) hypernetwork-based injection exhibits broadly predictive power law scaling along all architecture axes; and (ii) hypernetworks are capable of reliable OOD generalization at increasing scales, suggesting that hypernetwork provides a promising alternative to other train-time adaptation methods such as LoRA finetuning and full fine-tuning, exhibiting steeper scaling exponents in all OOD evaluations. Together, these results establish hypernetworks as a principled and scalable substrate for train-time adaptation, and provide the first empirically grounded scaling laws to guide hypernetworks for factual reasoning in large language models.

ru.title: Масштабируемое внедрение знаний в LLM через гиперсети
ru.desc: В работе исследуется использование гиперсетей для внедрения факт­ических знаний в большие языковые модели на этапе обучения. Авторы предлагают метод, при котором гиперсеть генерирует адаптер LoRA, который позволяет модели ответить на вопросы о миллионах фактов из большого корпуса. Впервые проведено систематическое изучение законов масштабирования гиперсетей, показано, что потери, точность рассуждений и обобщение на данные вне распределения следуют степенным законам. Результаты демонстрируют, что гиперсети обеспечивают более надёжное внедрение знаний с лучшими показателями масштабирования по сравнению с LoRA-дообучением и полным fine-tuning.

en.title: Hypernetworks: A Scalable Solution for Knowledge Injection in LLMs
en.desc: This paper explores the use of hypernetworks for injecting factual knowledge into large language models (LLMs) during training. By training a hypernetwork to generate a fixed LoRA adapter, the authors enable the target model to effectively answer questions based on a large corpus of facts. The study investigates how the performance of hypernetworks scales with their architecture, focusing on aspects like loss, reasoning accuracy, and out-of-distribution generalization. The findings suggest that hypernetworks offer a scalable and effective method for knowledge injection, outperforming traditional adaptation techniques.

zh.title: 超网络：大型语言模型的知识注入新方法
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了如何在训练过程中将事实知识有效地注入大型语言模型（LLMs）。我们提出使用超网络（hypernetworks）来生成固定的LoRA适配器，从而使模型能够回答关于这些事实的问题。通过构建一个包含数千万个多跳问答示例的大规模数据集MegaWikiQA，我们研究了超网络的注入能力与目标模型的通用能力之间的关系。研究结果表明，超网络在不同规模下能够可靠地进行知识注入，并在OOD（分布外）泛化方面表现出色，提供了一种有前景的训练时间适应方法。

papers.title: Beyond Euclidean Clipping: Overcoming Exploration Collapse in LLM RL via Riemannian Isometric Policy Optimization
papers.abstract: Abstract  Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a dominant paradigm for enhancing LLMs' reasoning capabilities. However, RL algorithms with PPO-Clip are inherently limited by exploration collapse. Subsequent works remain primarily heuristic and fail to identify the essential cause of PPO-Clip's failure. This work reveals the fundamental flaw of PPO-Clip: it implicitly measures policy discrepancy using Euclidean metric, which is theoretically inconsistent with the intrinsic geometry on the policy Riemannian manifold. This geometric mismatch results in overly conservative updates in low-probability regions while aggressive in high-probability regions, ultimately collapsing exploration. To correct this geometric flaw, we propose Riemannian Isometric Policy Optimization (RIPO), which guarantees isometric policy updates on the Riemannian manifold, effectively balancing exploration and exploitation. We further show that RIPO achieves a favorable bias-variance trade-off, which stabilizes optimization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RIPO significantly surpasses existing LLM RL algorithms across seven competition-level benchmarks (up to 60% improvement over GRPO on AIME24).
affiliations: Institute for AI Industry Research (AIR), Tsinghua University
SIA-Lab of Tsinghua AIR and ByteDance Seed

ru.title: Исправление геометрии: от евклидова пространства к риманову многообразию в обучении с подкреплением
ru.desc: В работе показано, что алгоритм PPO-Clip, широко используемый для обучения с подкреплением больших языковых моделей, имеет фундаментальный недостаток: он использует евклидову метрику для измерения различия политик, что противоречит геометрии риманова многообразия политик. Это геометрическое несоответствие приводит к тому, что обновления политики становятся слишком консервативными в областях низкой вероятности и чрезмерно агрессивными в областях высокой вероятности, что вызывает коллапс исследования пространства. Авторы предлагают метод RIPO (Riemannian Isometric Policy Optimization), который гарантирует изометрические обновления политики на риманове многообразии и правильно балансирует исследование и эксплуатацию. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют значительное превосходство RIPO над существующими алгоритмами обучения с подкреплением для LLM на конкурсных бенчмарках, включая улучшение на 60% по сравнению с GRPO на AIME24.

en.title: RIPO: Optimizing Reinforcement Learning with Geometric Precision
en.desc: This paper addresses a significant limitation in reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms, particularly those using PPO-Clip, which suffer from exploration collapse due to a geometric mismatch in policy updates. The authors identify that PPO-Clip measures policy discrepancy using the Euclidean metric, which does not align with the true geometry of the policy Riemannian manifold. To resolve this issue, they introduce Riemannian Isometric Policy Optimization (RIPO), which ensures that policy updates are consistent with the manifold's geometry, thus improving the balance between exploration and exploitation. Their experiments show that RIPO outperforms existing RL algorithms for large language models (LLMs), achieving up to a 60% improvement on benchmark tasks.

zh.title: 优化策略，平衡探索与利用
zh.desc: 强化学习（RL）已成为提升大型语言模型（LLM）推理能力的主要方法。然而，使用PPO-Clip的RL算法在探索方面存在固有的局限性。本文揭示了PPO-Clip的根本缺陷：它使用欧几里得度量来测量策略差异，这与策略黎曼流形的内在几何不一致。为了解决这一几何缺陷，我们提出了黎曼等距策略优化（RIPO），它在黎曼流形上保证等距的策略更新，有效平衡了探索与利用。

papers.title: Generalizable VLA Finetuning via Representation Anchoring and Language-Action Alignment
papers.abstract: Abstract  Finetuning a pretrained vision-language model (VLM) on robot demonstrations via behavior cloning (BC) has become the standard recipe for vision-language-action (VLA) policies. However, BC finetuning progressively overwrites the pretrained representations that support visual and semantic generalization. Co-training on web image-text data, a common remedy, does not prevent this; it applies language and action losses to separate observations, leaving VLAs with language-action misalignment that standard manipulation benchmarks do not expose. We propose Anchor-Align, which augments BC with two objectives: Vision-Language Anchoring distills layer-wise representations from a frozen VLM copy to prevent this drift, while Language-Action Alignment converts each action target into a discrete motion-direction label and jointly trains language and action prediction on the same robot observation. On a physical xArm7 robot, across two widely used VLA architectures, Anchor-Align improves real-robot success on both (28% to 54% and 37% to 60%). At scale in simulation, we demonstrate consistent improvements on OOD perturbations, perceptual robustness, and long-horizon control across LIBERO-PRO, LIBERO-Plus, and CALVIN, respectively, suggesting that preserving pretrained representations and effective action learning are not fundamentally at odds. Project page: anchoralignvla.github.io

ru.title: Сохранение знаний VLM при обучении робота управлению через согласование языка и действий
ru.desc: Статья предлагает метод Anchor-Align для улучшения обучения vision-language-action (VLA) моделей робота на основе поведенческого клонирования. Авторы выявили, что стандартное дообучение на демонстрациях приводит к деградации предобученных представлений видения и языка, а совместное обучение на web-данных не решает проблему языково-действенной несогласованности. Предлагаемый метод использует два механизма: дистилляцию представлений из замороженной модели для сохранения знаний VLM и совместное обучение предсказанию языка и направления движения на одних и тех же наблюдениях робота. Экспериментальные результаты на физическом роботе xArm7 и в симуляции демонстрируют значительное улучшение успешности выполнения задач и обобщения к новым условиям.

en.title: Anchor-Align: Enhancing Robot Learning with Vision-Language Harmony
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenge of finetuning vision-language models (VLMs) for robot demonstrations using behavior cloning (BC), which can overwrite important pretrained representations. The authors introduce a method called Anchor-Align that combines two strategies: Vision-Language Anchoring to maintain the integrity of pretrained representations, and Language-Action Alignment to ensure that language and action predictions are trained together on the same observations. By applying this approach, the researchers demonstrate significant improvements in the performance of robots on various tasks, achieving higher success rates in real-world scenarios. The results indicate that it is possible to preserve the benefits of pretrained models while effectively learning actions for robotic manipulation.

zh.title: Anchor-Align：提升机器人视觉-语言-动作学习的有效方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为Anchor-Align的方法，用于在机器人演示中微调预训练的视觉-语言模型（VLM）。该方法通过视觉-语言锚定和语言-动作对齐两个目标，防止了微调过程中预训练表示的漂移。实验表明，Anchor-Align在物理机器人xArm7上显著提高了成功率，并在模拟环境中展示了对外部扰动的鲁棒性。该研究表明，保持预训练表示和有效的动作学习并不矛盾。

papers.title: FVAttn: Adaptive Sparse Attention with Runtime Load Balancing for Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract  Video Diffusion Transformers process long spatio-temporal sequences, making self-attention the main bottleneck in high-resolution video generation. Training-free sparse attention reduces this cost, but adaptive Top-p routing creates uneven per-head workloads under multi-GPU sequence parallelism. The resulting workload heterogeneity turns sparse attention into a rank-level straggler problem. We present , a training-free sparse-attention system that improves the distributed execution efficiency of adaptive sparse attention under multi-GPU sequence parallelism.  uses Top-p routing, a Top-k safety floor, and video-aware block organization as the sparse-routing frontend, then repairs the materialized mask at runtime. Runtime Load Balancing migrates a small number of heavy heads via P2P communication to shorten the current critical path. Slack-Aware Sparse Augmentation fills residual non-critical-rank slack with additional high-value blocks, while overlap hides scheduling and migration overhead behind existing computation. On step-distilled Wan2.2 I2V,  reduces average load imbalance from 1.34 to 1.08 and delivers a 4.41times attention speedup over FlashAttention, while achieving a 2.02--2.11times DiT inference speedup with competitive video quality.

ru.title: Балансировка нагрузки в разреженном attention для распределённой генерации видео
ru.desc: В статье представляется система для оптимизации механизма self-attention в Video Diffusion Transformers при обработке длинных видеопоследовательностей. Авторы используют адаптивную разреженную attention с Top-p маршрутизацией, которая снижает вычислительную нагрузку, но создаёт неравномерное распределение работы между GPU при параллелизме последовательностей. Предложенный подход включает балансировку нагрузки во время выполнения, миграцию тяжёлых голов через P2P коммуникацию и умное заполнение простоев дополнительными блоками. Система достигает 4.41-кратного ускорения attention операций и 2.02-2.11-кратного ускорения инференса видеомодели при сохранении качества генерируемых видео.

en.title: Boosting Video Generation Efficiency with Smart Sparse Attention
en.desc: This paper introduces a new system for improving the efficiency of sparse attention mechanisms in video generation using multi-GPU setups. It addresses the issue of workload imbalance caused by adaptive Top-p routing, which can lead to delays in processing. The proposed method includes techniques like runtime load balancing and slack-aware sparse augmentation to optimize the distribution of computational tasks. As a result, the system achieves significant speed improvements in attention processing while maintaining high video quality.

zh.title: 提升视频生成效率的稀疏注意力系统
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种训练无关的稀疏注意力系统，旨在提高多GPU序列并行下自适应稀疏注意力的分布式执行效率。通过使用Top-p路由和Top-k安全底线，系统能够有效组织视频块并在运行时修复物化掩码。运行时负载平衡技术通过点对点通信迁移少量重负载头，以缩短当前的关键路径。最终，系统在保持竞争性视频质量的同时，实现了显著的注意力加速和推理速度提升。

papers.title: Trace: A Taxonomy-Guided Environment for Multidomain Visual Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract  Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has substantially improved language-model reasoning, yet its extension to vision-language models remains constrained by the lack of training data that are simultaneously broad, exactly verifiable, and reproducible. We introduce Trace, a taxonomy-guided environment for multidomain visual reasoning. Trace factorizes task construction into a scene grammar and an executable task program, separating visual realization from answer computation. A shared semantic state determines the rendered image, prompt, typed answer, verifier state, and replayable instance trace. The resulting environment comprises 1,000 tasks over 277 scene grammars and 11 visual domains, with controlled semantic and visual variation. RLVR on 64,000 Trace instances improves the macro-average across 24 external benchmarks by 3.51 percentage points for Qwen2.5-VL-3B and 4.06 points for Qwen2.5-VL-7B, providing evidence that broad procedural training can transfer beyond the generated task distributions. Project page: https://maveryn.github.io/trace/.

ru.title: Процедурно сгенерированные данные для обучения видео-языковых моделей с верификацией
ru.desc: В работе представлена Trace — специализированная среда для обучения видео-языковых моделей с проверяемыми наградами. Среда использует таксономию и отделяет визуальное представление сцены от вычисления ответов через единое семантическое состояние. Она содержит 1000 задач из 277 грамматик сцен и 11 визуальных доменов с контролируемой вариацией. Обучение на 64 000 примерах из Trace значительно улучшило производительность моделей Qwen2.5-VL на внешних бенчмарках благодаря хорошей обобщаемости процедурно сгенерированных данных.

en.title: Enhancing Visual Reasoning with Trace: A New Training Environment
en.desc: This paper presents Trace, a new environment designed to enhance visual reasoning in vision-language models using reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). Trace organizes tasks into a structured format using scene grammar and executable task programs, which helps in separating the visual aspects from the computation of answers. The environment includes a diverse set of 1,000 tasks across various scene grammars and visual domains, ensuring a wide range of training scenarios. The results show that training on Trace significantly improves performance on external benchmarks, demonstrating the effectiveness of broad procedural training in transferring knowledge to different tasks.

zh.title: Trace：多领域视觉推理的新突破
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为Trace的多领域视觉推理环境，旨在解决视觉语言模型训练数据不足的问题。Trace通过场景语法和可执行任务程序将任务构建分解，从而将视觉实现与答案计算分开。该环境包含1000个任务，涵盖277种场景语法和11个视觉领域，具有可控的语义和视觉变化。通过在64,000个Trace实例上进行强化学习，Qwen2.5-VL-3B和Qwen2.5-VL-7B的性能在24个外部基准测试中分别提高了3.51和4.06个百分点，证明了广泛的程序性训练可以超越生成的任务分布。

papers.title: SLPO: Scaling Latent Reasoning via a Surrogate Policy
papers.abstract: Abstract  Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has become the predominant recipe for eliciting test-time scaling in explicit Chain-of-Thought reasoners. Yet this scaling path remains computationally costly, since every intermediate step must be decoded as a language token. Latent reasoning instead carries intermediate computation as continuous vectors and already matches or surpasses explicit CoT at far shorter horizons. Despite this promise, latent reasoners remain largely imitation-bound, while explicit CoT has already moved past imitation via outcome-reward RL. Latent trajectories lack a tractable per-step likelihood and an adaptive stopping interface under fixed thinking budgets, so outcome rewards cannot elicit latent test-time scaling. We introduce Surrogate Latent Policy Optimization (SLPO) to bring outcome-reward RL to autoregressive latent reasoners: an empirical surrogate policy density over latent transitions for trajectory-level credit assignment, and a correctness-supervised stopping head that outcome-reward optimization refines into a variable-horizon policy. Across continuous and soft thinking settings, SLPO improves Pass@k under parallel sampling and allocates longer latent computation to harder instances with higher deterministic accuracy.

ru.title: Скрытые рассуждения с подкреплением: масштабирование без явного декодирования
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод Surrogate Latent Policy Optimization (SLPO), который применяет обучение с подкреплением на основе вознаграждений к латентным рассуждениям в языковых моделях. Вместо явной цепочки рассуждений (Chain-of-Thought), где каждый промежуточный шаг кодируется токеном, латентные рассуждения обрабатывают вычисления как непрерывные векторы, что значительно снижает вычислительные затраты. Авторы вводят суррогатную плотность политики для распределения кредита по латентной траектории и головку управления остановкой, которая позволяет моделировать переменный горизонт рассуждений. Метод SLPO улучшает качество генерации и адаптивно выделяет больше вычислительных ресурсов на сложные примеры.

en.title: Optimizing Latent Reasoning with SLPO for Efficient Learning
en.desc: This paper discusses a new method called Surrogate Latent Policy Optimization (SLPO) for improving reinforcement learning in latent reasoning systems. Traditional methods use explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning, which is computationally expensive due to the need to decode every step as a language token. SLPO allows for more efficient reasoning by using continuous vectors for intermediate computations, enabling faster and more effective learning. The method enhances the ability of latent reasoners to adaptively allocate computational resources based on the complexity of tasks, leading to better performance in various reasoning scenarios.

zh.title: 引入替代潜在策略优化，提升潜在推理器的性能
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了使用可验证奖励的强化学习在显式思维链推理器中的应用。尽管这种方法在计算上成本高昂，因为每个中间步骤都必须解码为语言标记，但潜在推理方法通过使用连续向量来进行中间计算，能够在更短的时间内达到或超过显式思维链的效果。论文提出了一种新的方法，称为替代潜在策略优化（SLPO），旨在将结果奖励强化学习引入自回归潜在推理器。通过这种方法，SLPO在并行采样下提高了性能，并为更复杂的实例分配了更长的潜在计算时间。

papers.title: DocOps: A Verifiable Benchmark for Autonomous Agents in Complex Document Operations
papers.abstract: Abstract  As autonomous agents rapidly evolve, their ability to reliably manipulate ubiquitous digital documents has become critical for enabling general-purpose AI assistants and automating complex workspace workflows. In this paper, we introduce DocOps, a deterministically verifiable evaluation framework underpinned by a hierarchical taxonomy that deconstructs document operations inspired by real-world practices into atomic dimensions and escalating workflow complexities. Based on DocOps, we systematically evaluate representative closed- and open-source models across various agentic harnesses, revealing that even the most advanced frontier configurations still exhibit profound limitations when handling highly coupled, long-range tasks. Furthermore, a fine-grained analysis of existing agents' manipulation behaviors uncovers 3 key failure modes: long-term state tracking collapse, shallow semantic verification, and destructive editing of structural metadata. Ultimately, our work exposes the capability boundaries of agents in maintaining global document consistency, shedding light on the future design of robust, non-destructive agents for complex digital ecosystems.
affiliations: Chinese Information Processing Laboratory Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Границы способностей агентов в манипулировании сложными документами
ru.desc: В работе представлена DocOps — это фреймворк для оценки способности автономных агентов манипулировать цифровыми документами, построенный на основе иерархической таксономии операций. Авторы систематически оценили закрытые и открытые модели на различных архитектурах агентов и выявили, что даже продвинутые модели имеют серьёзные ограничения при работе со сложными, длительными задачами. Детальный анализ показал три ключевых типа ошибок: потеря отслеживания состояния, поверхностная семантическая верификация и деструктивное редактирование метаданных документа. Исследование выявляет критические границы способностей агентов в сохранении глобальной консистентности документов.

en.title: Enhancing Document Handling in AI Agents with DocOps
en.desc: This paper presents DocOps, a framework designed to evaluate how well autonomous agents can handle digital documents. It breaks down document operations into smaller parts, allowing for a detailed analysis of how these agents perform in various tasks. The study finds that even the best models struggle with complex tasks that require long-term tracking and accurate editing. The authors identify three main issues that lead to failures in document manipulation, highlighting the need for better designs in future AI agents.

zh.title: 揭示智能体在文档处理中的能力边界
zh.desc: 随着自主智能体的快速发展，它们可靠地处理数字文档的能力变得至关重要。本文介绍了DocOps，这是一个基于分层分类法的可验证评估框架，旨在将文档操作分解为基本维度和逐步复杂的工作流程。通过DocOps，我们系统地评估了各种代理模型，发现即使是最先进的配置在处理复杂任务时仍存在显著局限性。我们的研究揭示了现有智能体在文档一致性维护方面的能力边界，为未来设计强大且无破坏性的智能体提供了启示。

papers.title: Train the Model, Not the Reader: Decodability Supervision for Verifiable Activation Explanations
papers.abstract: Abstract  Natural-language autoencoders score explanations of hidden activations by reconstruction: an explanation is deemed faithful if the activation can be regenerated from it. The test is structurally insensitive to individual false claims: if flipping a claim does not change the reconstruction, the claim is never penalized. We show the test is passed in two ways, neither faithful. On a released Qwen-2.5-7B verbalizer, explanations reconstruct well above chance while ~2% of specific claims are reconstruction-dependent, so the score tracks gist, not specific facts. Under exact synthetic ground truth, the standard recipe develops co-adapted private codes (false wording the reconstruction depends on) in 5/5 runs, and fixes that leave the target model unchanged do not help. We contribute two audit protocols, the grounded-vs-true cross and the evaluator swap, and RECAP (Readable Encodings via Co-trained Auxiliary Predictors): linear heads trained alongside the target model to keep designated content decodable. On RECAP-trained sandbox models, fresh verbalizers state the designated content truly and the codes vanish, at a +0.001-nat cost. This replicates on a pretrained Pythia-160M: the content becomes reliably probe-decodable, though a fresh verbalizer conveys it only in part (truth 0.44-0.46 vs a near-zero control). For interpretability, high reconstruction does not certify individual claims. For AI safety, RECAP makes designated internal content independently checkable against probes rather than asserted by prose a model can game: an independent probe scores the verbalizer's true claims above its false ones (AUC 0.96, vs 0.82 without RECAP). Against an adversary that edits an explanation to maximize the reconstruction score while lying (suppressing ~87% of its lie penalty), the RECAP probe still flags the lies (AUC 0.95) while the control probe collapses to chance (0.51).

ru.title: Надёжные объяснения через независимую проверку внутреннего содержимого моделей
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема надёжности объяснений скрытых активаций нейросетей, основанных на реконструкции. Авторы показывают, что высокий балл реконструкции не гарантирует верность отдельных утверждений в объяснениях, так как модели могут разработать приватные коды, которые скрывают ложные факты. Для решения этой проблемы предложен метод RECAP, использующий вспомогательные линейные классификаторы, обученные совместно с основной моделью, чтобы сделать внутреннее содержимое независимо проверяемым. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что RECAP значительно улучшает обнаружение ложных утверждений в объяснениях, даже против адаптивных противников.

en.title: Ensuring Truth in AI Explanations with RECAP
en.desc: This paper discusses a method for evaluating the faithfulness of explanations generated by natural-language autoencoders. It highlights that traditional reconstruction tests can overlook inaccuracies in explanations, as they may not penalize false claims if they do not affect the overall reconstruction. The authors introduce RECAP, a technique that uses co-trained auxiliary predictors to ensure that the internal content of models is accurately represented and can be independently verified. Their findings show that while high reconstruction scores do not guarantee the truth of individual claims, RECAP significantly improves the reliability of the explanations against adversarial manipulation.

zh.title: 提高模型解释可信度的新方法
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了自然语言自动编码器如何通过重建隐藏激活的解释来评估其可信度。研究表明，虽然重建得分高，但并不意味着解释是准确的，因为许多具体的声明并不影响重建结果。作者提出了两种审计协议和一种新的方法RECAP，以提高模型内部内容的可检查性。通过RECAP训练的模型能够更可靠地传达指定内容，并且在面对对手时，仍能有效识别虚假声明。

papers.title: G-MAD: A Game-Based Data Generation Framework for Multi-View RGB-T Aerial Object Detection
papers.abstract: Abstract  This work introduces G-MAD, an open-source framework that uses Arma3 to generate synchronized multi-view RGB-T data for aerial object detection. G-MAD addresses key limitations of real-world aerial dataset construction, including limited viewpoint control, imperfect RGB-T alignment and high annotation cost. The framework supports structured scenario specification, controllable multi-view camera placement, simultaneous visible/thermal capture, and automatic bounding box annotation using engine-level geometric metadata. These capabilities enable controlled studies of viewpoint variation, multi-modal fusion, and synthetic-to-real transfer in aerial object detection. Besides, using G-MAD, we construct and release AMOD, a new large-scale multi-view aerial RGB-T object detection benchmark. The source code and the dataset are available at https://unique-chan.github.io/G-MAD-Project.

ru.title: Синтетические данные для детекции объектов с воздуха: фреймворк мультивидовой генерации RGB-T
ru.desc: Статья представляет G-MAD, открытый фреймворк для синтеза мультивидовых RGB-T данных с использованием игрового движка Arma3, решающий проблемы создания датасетов для детекции объектов с беспилотников. Фреймворк позволяет контролировать позиции камер, одновременно захватывать видимый и тепловой спектры и автоматически аннотировать бounding box'ы используя геометрические метаданные движка. На базе G-MAD авторы создали новый крупномасштабный бенчмарк AMOD для мультивидовой аэросъемки с мультимодальной информацией, что дает возможность контролировать влияние вариации точек обзора и качества синтетических данных на обобщение моделей.

en.title: G-MAD: Revolutionizing Aerial Object Detection with Multi-View RGB-T Data
en.desc: G-MAD is an innovative framework designed to generate synchronized multi-view RGB-T (Red, Green, Blue, and Thermal) data specifically for aerial object detection tasks. It overcomes challenges faced in real-world dataset creation, such as limited control over viewpoints, misalignment of RGB and thermal images, and the high costs associated with manual annotation. The framework allows users to specify scenarios, control camera placements, and automatically annotate data using geometric metadata, facilitating research in viewpoint variation and multi-modal data fusion. Additionally, G-MAD has led to the creation of AMOD, a new benchmark dataset for evaluating aerial RGB-T object detection, which is publicly available for further research.

zh.title: G-MAD：空中物体检测的新工具
zh.desc: 本文介绍了G-MAD，这是一个开源框架，利用Arma3生成同步的多视角RGB-T数据，用于空中物体检测。G-MAD解决了现实世界空中数据集构建中的关键限制，包括有限的视角控制、不完美的RGB-T对齐和高昂的标注成本。该框架支持结构化场景规范、可控的多视角相机放置、同时捕获可见光和热成像，并使用引擎级几何元数据进行自动边界框标注。这些功能使得在空中物体检测中能够进行视角变化、多模态融合和合成到真实的转移研究。

papers.title: SeededGrasp: Language-Guided Grasping in Complex Scenes with Multiple Embodiments
papers.abstract: Abstract  Practical robotic grasping in complex scenes requires both 3D spatial reasoning and alignment with task-specific requirements. Vision-language models (VLMs) offer a natural way to specify these requirements using language, but existing approaches either use a VLM to predict the grasp directly with limited spatial awareness, or train the VLM together with the grasping model, which requires significantly more data and compute. These limitations impede performance and have prevented scaling to multiple embodiments in complex scenes. We address this by proposing SeededGrasp, a novel data-efficient framework that enables a VLM to predict a seed point to be used as conditioning for a subsequent lightweight grasp-generation model. Our architecture decouples high-level semantic reasoning from low-level geometric execution, enabling multi-embodiment support while bypassing the need for expensive end-to-end training. To enable training such models, we release the first multi-embodiment tabletop grasping dataset comprising over 2.5M grasps in cluttered scenes. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms existing baselines, achieving 72% success in simulation and 78% in real-world grasping experiments. See our project site for data and code: https://uoft-isl.github.io/seeded-grasp/

ru.title: Разделение семантики и геометрии для эффективного робототехнического захвата
ru.desc: В работе предлагается SeededGrasp — фреймворк для захвата объектов роботом, который разделяет задачу на два этапа: модель зрения и языка предсказывает семантически значимую точку, а легковесная модель генерирует захват на основе этой точки. Такой подход позволяет эффективно использовать данные и избежать дорогостоящего сквозного обучения, достигая при этом лучшей производительности. Авторы выпустили первый мультиагентный датасет для захвата объектов, содержащий более 2.5 миллионов примеров в сложных сценах. Метод показал 72% успеха в симуляции и 78% в реальных экспериментах с роботом.

en.title: Efficient Grasping with Seeded Points and Vision-Language Models
en.desc: This paper introduces SeededGrasp, a new framework for robotic grasping that combines vision-language models (VLMs) with a lightweight grasp-generation model. Instead of directly predicting grasps, SeededGrasp uses a VLM to identify a seed point, which helps guide the grasping process while maintaining spatial awareness. This approach separates high-level reasoning from low-level execution, allowing for efficient training and support for multiple robotic embodiments. The authors also provide a large dataset of over 2.5 million grasps in cluttered environments to facilitate the training of their model, showing significant improvements in grasping success rates in both simulations and real-world scenarios.

zh.title: 高效抓取：SeededGrasp框架的创新
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为SeededGrasp的新框架，旨在提高复杂场景中机器人的抓取能力。该框架利用视觉-语言模型（VLM）预测一个种子点，作为后续轻量级抓取生成模型的条件，从而实现高效的数据利用。通过将高层语义推理与低层几何执行解耦，SeededGrasp支持多种抓取方式，避免了昂贵的端到端训练需求。实验结果表明，该方法在模拟和真实世界抓取实验中均优于现有基线，成功率分别达到72%和78%。

papers.title: ATSplat: Compact Feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting with Adaptive Token Expansion
papers.abstract: Abstract  3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) achieves high-quality novel-view synthesis by optimizing freely placed primitives in 3D and adaptively densifying them in under-reconstructed regions. However, this scene-adaptive capacity allocation is largely lost in existing feed-forward 3DGS methods, which commonly regress Gaussians at input pixels and lift them along camera rays. Such pixel-aligned formulations make the number and placement of primitives depend on image resolution and input viewpoints rather than scene complexity, resulting in dense and often redundant Gaussian sets. We present ATSplat, a feed-forward 3DGS framework that restores the adaptive allocation capability of 3DGS optimization through Adaptive 3D Tokens. ATSplat first lifts coarse patch-level depth and camera cues into sparse 3D anchor tokens, forming a compact scaffold of the scene. Each token is then regressed into local Gaussians with learnable 3D offsets, decoupling primitive placement from input image grids. An Adaptive Token Expansion module predicts a token-level uncertainty score, supervised by rendering error maps, and selectively expands high-uncertainty tokens through learnable expansion layers. This sparse-to-adaptive formulation enables ATSplat to concentrate primitives in challenging regions while maintaining a compact representation. Experiments on two representative datasets, RealEstate10K and DL3DV, show that ATSplat achieves state-of-the-art rendering quality while reducing the number of Gaussians by more than 5.7times compared with dense feed-forward 3DGS methods. From 12 input images at 512 times 960 resolution, ATSplat completes reconstruction in less than a second using a single commercial GPU, and renders high-quality novel views at 1136 FPS (512 times 960) with only 311K Gaussians.

ru.title: Адаптивные токены для эффективного синтеза 3D сцен
ru.desc: В статье представлен ATSplat — метод быстрого синтеза новых видов сцены на основе 3D Gaussian Splatting, который адаптивно распределяет примитивы в зависимости от сложности сцены, а не от разрешения входного изображения. Авторы вводят концепцию адаптивных 3D токенов, которые образуют разреженный скелет сцены на основе глубины и информации камеры, позволяя избежать избыточного количества гауссиан. Модуль адаптивного расширения предсказывает неуверенность для каждого токена и селективно расширяет области с высокой ошибкой, концентрируя примитивы в сложных регионах. Метод достигает качественного синтеза с 5.7 раза меньшим количеством гауссиан и рендерит сцены на скорости 1136 FPS с использованием одного GPU.

en.title: Adaptive 3D Tokens for Efficient Novel-View Synthesis
en.desc: The paper introduces ATSplat, a novel framework for 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) that enhances the quality of novel-view synthesis by optimizing the placement of 3D primitives. Unlike traditional methods that rely on pixel alignment, ATSplat uses Adaptive 3D Tokens to create a sparse representation of the scene, allowing for more efficient and adaptive allocation of resources based on scene complexity. This approach reduces redundancy by concentrating Gaussian primitives in areas that require more detail, while maintaining a compact overall representation. Experimental results demonstrate that ATSplat significantly improves rendering quality and efficiency, achieving state-of-the-art performance with fewer Gaussians compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 自适应3D标记，提升高斯点云合成质量
zh.desc: 3D高斯点云（3DGS）通过优化自由放置的原语来实现高质量的新视角合成，但现有的前馈3DGS方法在场景自适应能力分配上存在不足。ATSplat是一个前馈3DGS框架，通过自适应3D标记恢复了这种能力。它首先将粗糙的深度和相机线索提升为稀疏的3D锚标记，然后将每个标记回归为具有可学习3D偏移的局部高斯，从而使原语的放置与输入图像网格解耦。实验表明，ATSplat在保持紧凑表示的同时，能够在复杂区域集中原语，并且在渲染质量上达到了最先进的水平。
